Strengthen bb-cli skill triggers

feat: add admin entitlements and shared project improvements

- backend:
  - add admin email allowlist parsing, user-email auth payloads, and a me/entitlements endpoint
  - enforce admin unlimited quota by bypassing exhaustion checks and skipping post-run usage increments
  - keep websocket generation/chat flow aligned with authenticated user context and start-generation args
- frontend:
  - add shared project client flow for /p/[slug] with owner/public modes and read-only behavior
  - add admin entitlement fetch and show unlimited quota labels in dashboard, top bar, and questionnaire
  - preserve chat streaming and pipeline UX while integrating owner/public and quota-state controls
- database:
  - add migrations to make projects public by default and remove client profile self-update policy
- tests:
  - add admin allowlist and generation-service admin quota tests
  - extend generation-start and websocket tests for auth user payloads and entitlements behavior

Closes #17
Closes #19
Closes #31

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(codex): enforce completion comments via dynamic tools

Summary:
- add a typed Linear issue comment path and require a completion
  comment before a Codex turn is accepted
- advertise and handle dynamic Linear tools using the current
  app-server request and response schema
- update workflow guidance and regression tests for the completion
  comment contract

Rationale:
- prompt-only guidance was not enough to guarantee that completed
  tickets received a Linear summary comment
- the real Codex app-server protocol differs from the earlier fake
  test shape, so the runtime needed schema-compliant dynamic tool
  requests and responses

Tests:
- npm test -- test/codex/app-server.test.ts test/codex/agent-runner.test.ts
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
HEL-15: accessibility and responsive pass for v1 calculator UI (#7)

* feat(pages): add deployable run pace calculator site

Summary:
- add a static pace calculator UI with linked pace, speed, and race
  finish projections
- add pure calculation helpers with Node tests, including a DOM smoke
  test for the page wiring
- add a GitHub Pages deployment workflow and document the production URL

Rationale:
- main did not contain an existing calculator implementation, so this
  branch ships the smallest complete site that satisfies the Pages
  deployment ticket
- keeping the app dependency-free makes the Pages artifact simple and
  reduces deployment risk for a public static site

Tests:
- npm test
- node --check script.js
- node --check calculator.js
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(readme): note GitHub Pages repo setup

Summary:
- document that pushes to main deploy through the GitHub Pages workflow
- add the one-time GitHub Pages setting needed on the repository

Rationale:
- the remaining completion step happens in GitHub, so the repo should
  state the exact Pages configuration maintainers need to enable

Tests:
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(deploy): add GitHub Pages publish checklist

Summary:
- add a repo-local deployment runbook for the GitHub Pages workflow
- link the README deployment section to the runbook

Rationale:
- the remaining ticket steps happen outside this sandbox, so the repo
  should contain a clear publish and verification checklist for the
  next environment

Tests:
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* ci(pages): verify deployed site after publish

Summary:
- add a post-deploy check that fetches the GitHub Pages page_url
- fail the workflow if the deployed site does not return the expected
  calculator content
- document the automated live verification in the README and deployment
  runbook

Rationale:
- this moves the production URL verification into GitHub Actions so the
  Pages deployment ticket can validate the live site automatically once
  the branch is merged

Tests:
- npm test
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* ci(pages): extract live site verification script

Summary:
- add a reusable script for verifying deployed GitHub Pages content
- update the Pages workflow to call the script after deployment
- document the script-based verification step in the deployment runbook

Rationale:
- extracting the live verification into a script keeps the workflow
  maintainable and gives maintainers a reusable command for checking the
  published site outside GitHub Actions

Tests:
- npm test
- bash -n scripts/verify-pages-content.sh
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* build(tooling): standardize validation scripts

Summary:
- add npm scripts for syntax checks, local validation, and live Pages
  verification
- update the GitHub Pages workflow to call the shared npm scripts
- update the README and deployment runbook to use the standardized
  commands

Rationale:
- using the same commands in local docs and GitHub Actions reduces
  handoff ambiguity and keeps the final publish step repeatable in the
  next environment

Tests:
- npm run validate
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(readme): fix GitHub-facing markdown links

Summary:
- replace workspace-absolute markdown links in the README with
  repo-relative links
- replace the deployment runbook's workflow link with a repo-relative
  path

Rationale:
- absolute filesystem links work only in the local sandbox and break on
  GitHub, so the production-facing docs need repository-relative links

Tests:
- npm run validate
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* ci(pages): bound live site verification requests

Summary:
- add connection and request timeouts to the Pages verification script

Rationale:
- this prevents the future post-deploy verification step from hanging
  indefinitely if the published site is unavailable or slow to respond

Tests:
- npm run validate
- bash -n scripts/verify-pages-content.sh

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* build(pages): extract static artifact packaging script

Summary:
- add a reusable build script for producing the Pages `dist/` artifact
- add `npm run build` and update the Pages workflow to use it
- ignore generated build output and document the build command in the
  deployment runbook

Rationale:
- using the same packaging command locally and in GitHub Actions reduces
  release drift and makes the final publish step easier to reproduce in
  the next environment

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm run validate
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* build(pages): verify packaged artifact contents

Summary:
- make the dist build script assert that each expected Pages artifact
  file exists after packaging

Rationale:
- this makes local and CI packaging fail fast if the static artifact is
  incomplete before upload to GitHub Pages

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm run validate

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* build(pages): fold artifact build into validation

Summary:
- make `npm run validate` produce the Pages `dist/` artifact after the
  test and syntax checks pass
- simplify the GitHub Pages workflow to upload the validated `dist/`
  directory directly

Rationale:
- this keeps local validation and CI artifact generation on the same
  path and removes one extra packaging step from the workflow

Tests:
- npm run validate
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(deploy): remove redundant build step

Summary:
- remove the standalone `npm run build` step from the deployment
  runbook now that `npm run validate` already produces the Pages
  artifact

Rationale:
- this keeps the documented publish flow aligned with the current npm
  scripts and avoids duplicate local steps

Tests:
- npm run validate

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(deploy): separate live verification from local checks

Summary:
- move the `npm run verify:pages` command out of the pre-push
  validation section in the deployment runbook

Rationale:
- the live Pages verification command only makes sense after a deployed
  production URL exists, so the runbook should not present it as a
  local pre-push check

Tests:
- npm run validate

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* build(tooling): validate shell scripts explicitly

Summary:
- add a shared `npm run check:shell` command for the Pages helper
  scripts
- make `npm run validate` run the shell syntax checks before building
  the deploy artifact

Rationale:
- this ensures the local and CI validation path covers the full shell
  tooling used by the GitHub Pages build and verification steps

Tests:
- npm run validate
- python3 -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(".github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml")); print("workflow-ok")'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(readme): note that validate builds dist

Summary:
- clarify in the README and deployment runbook that `npm run validate`
  also produces the Pages `dist/` artifact

Rationale:
- this makes the current validation flow explicit and keeps the
  handoff publish steps consistent with the repo scripts

Tests:
- npm run validate

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(ui): improve calculator accessibility and mobile layout

Summary:
- add visible per-control labels for every calculator input and select
- surface invalid pace, speed, and distance entries with text messages,
  aria-invalid state, and a live status summary
- collapse the form layout for narrow screens and add acceptance tests for
  labels, DOM order, and responsive CSS constraints

Rationale:
- the shipped calculator branch still relied on group labels and silent
  invalid-input handling, which left keyboard and screen-reader users with
  weak feedback
- the v1 release also needed explicit safeguards for 320 px layouts while
  preserving the existing calculator flow and output model

Tests:
- npm run validate
- attempted Playwright 320 px verification, but Chromium could not launch
  here because libnspr4.so is unavailable for the current user

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* ci(pages): verify deployed site matches the build

Summary:
- add a reusable script that compares the deployed GitHub Pages files
  to the local dist artifact
- update the Pages workflow to validate before upload and verify the
  published site after deployment
- document the production URL and release flow in the README and a
  deployment runbook

Rationale:
- main already publishes the calculator, but HEL-11 still lacked the
  production URL in the repository documentation
- comparing the live Pages output to the built artifact closes the
  remaining acceptance gap and keeps future main deployments checked

Tests:
- npm run validate
- npm run verify:pages -- http://localhost:4173/
- npm run verify:pages -- https://helionaut.github.io/run-pace-calculator/

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
HEL-14: preserve exact presets across shared unit switches (#6)

* ci(pages): verify deployed site matches the build

Summary:
- add a reusable script that compares the deployed GitHub Pages files
  to the local dist artifact
- update the Pages workflow to validate before upload and verify the
  published site after deployment
- document the production URL and release flow in the README and a
  deployment runbook

Rationale:
- main already publishes the calculator, but HEL-11 still lacked the
  production URL in the repository documentation
- comparing the live Pages output to the built artifact closes the
  remaining acceptance gap and keeps future main deployments checked

Tests:
- npm run validate
- npm run verify:pages -- http://localhost:4173/
- npm run verify:pages -- https://helionaut.github.io/run-pace-calculator/

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(calculator): preserve exact preset unit switching

Summary:
- keep canonical distance and speed values in shared calculator state
  so km/mi switches do not drift finish projections
- preserve named preset distances exactly across unit toggles and
  reset distance presets to Custom after manual edits
- add reducer coverage for finish stability, preset retention, and
  exact canonical preset miles on the current hel-11 base

Rationale:
- the latest hel-11 branch already includes the shared unit/preset UI,
  so HEL-14 needs targeted reducer fixes rather than a DOM rewrite
- storing exact canonical values behind rounded input displays keeps
  the UI readable while preserving mathematically stable results

Tests:
- npm test
- npm run check
- npm run validate

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Eugene Nikolskiy <4658906+helionaut@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add context-aware streaming chat agent

commit.message:
Expose PR task counts in status output

Co-Authored-By: Codex by OpenAI <codex@openai.com>

cooperator: harden submission spawn-failure completion semantics

Why:
- Submission draining could drop completion signaling when Spawn fails, which can strand SubmitSync callers and leak queued submission ownership under shutdown/allocation-pressure races.

What changed:
- Extended SubmissionEntry with completion-success plumbing for synchronous submit paths.
- Updated SubmitSync to return whether queued work actually spawned and completed.
- Hardened SpawnFromSubmission failure path to destroy entries and release completion semaphores when Spawn fails.
- Hardened DrainRemainingSubmissions to mark synchronous submissions unsuccessful before release.
- Added regression test  using an impossible stack-size request to exercise spawn-failure fast-return behavior.

Validation:
- cmake --build /tmp/coop-build -j 8 --target coop_tests
- /tmp/coop-build/bin/coop_tests --gtest_filter=ShutdownTest.*:SpawnTest.*

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: backend-owned generation with resilient observability

backend:
- decouple generation execution from websocket lifecycle via HTTP start endpoint and project subscriptions
- add persistent project store/runtime orchestration with generation status, stage, trace id, and incremental artifact writes
- emit detailed coder lifecycle pipeline events with elapsed timing, bounded token usage, and timeout/parse fallback paths
- expand backend test coverage for websocket contracts, generation start endpoint, coder progress, and project store behavior

frontend:
- add ws-aware fallback polling with adaptive backoff for /p/[slug] and stop polling on terminal states
- improve project refresh dedupe so generation-only field changes trigger UI updates
- add terraform progress UX (status text, progress bar, delayed guidance) driven by coder pipeline events
- enrich debug reporting with poll mode and coder milestones and thread new pipeline/terraform progress state through pages

database:
- add generation observability columns and indexes for projects in migration 003_generation_observability

dev:
- exclude virtualenv/cache paths from backend reload watcher to reduce local dev churn

Closes #16

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

Add Agent Skills for Elastic overview page (#5440)

## Summary

- Adds a new overview page at
`explore-analyze/ai-features/agent-skills.md` describing official
Elastic agent skills for AI coding agents.
- Content based on the
[elastic/agent-skills](https://github.com/elastic/agent-skills) README
and the [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) open standard.
- Covers what agent skills are, scope (Elastic stack coverage),
installation (npx and local clone), supported agents, skill format, and
update workflows.
- Adds the page to the `explore-analyze/toc.yml` under AI-powered
features.

No skill catalogue is included yet — this is the initial overview page
only.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Edu González de la Herrán <25320357+eedugon@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: tracking_engine
repository.description: Football tracking engine that spatially bins, time-buckets, and flattens Genius Sports JSONL tracking files into compressed Parquet. Processing 12 tracking files (matches) takes only 60 seconds. It’s fast because it stays columnar with Polars, skips optional derived helpers when disabled, and writes compressed row-grouped files that reduce I/O.

test(hardware): add transport-aware ring coverage

Summary:
- add hardware transport profile helpers for raw, udp, and
  optional redundant raw coverage
- generate the single-link hardware ring tests once per available
  transport and add a separate redundant ring suite
- document the env vars and --include hardware commands for the
  new transport matrix

Rationale:
- let the same hardware ring assertions run in raw or udp mode
  without duplicating the test body
- keep redundant coverage opt-in and explicit so benches with a
  backup link can exercise that path separately

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/master/config_test.exs
  test/integration/hardware/ring_test.exs
  test/integration/hardware/redundant_ring_test.exs

commit.message:
phase(1): Fix all Codex-identified bugs

## Bugs Fixed (8 total)

### High Severity (2)
- Fix import: refresh_all_decay_scores now implemented
- Fix TTL: extend_ttl() respects expiry, no blind revival

### Medium Severity (3)
- Remove duplicate DecayConfig (single source of truth)
- Use timezone-aware datetime (utcnow helper)
- Fix get_half_life() for non-decay layers (returns None)

### Low Severity (3)
- Fix TTL boundary check (< → <=)
- Improve decay formula (fractional days, clamp access)
- Use relative imports

## Changes
- synapse/layers/types.py: Full rewrite with entities, relations
- synapse/layers/decay.py: Bug fixes + new functions
- synapse/__init__.py: Clean exports
- docs/reports/phase_1_report.md: This report

Report: docs/reports/phase_1_report.md

commit.message:
ci: consolidate test workflow onto raw-capable runner

Summary:
- move the main CI test job from `ubuntu-latest` to `ubuntu-24.04`
- remove the separate raw simulator job and fold its veth setup and
  `CAP_NET_RAW` grant into the single remaining test job
- run the full test suite once on the raw-capable runner instead of
  splitting generic and raw coverage across two jobs

Rationale:
- the workflow no longer needs two runner paths now that the raw-socket
  capable runner can host the entire suite
- keeping one CI runner reduces duplication in checkout, beam setup,
  cache, and dependency install steps while preserving raw coverage

Validation:
- `ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/ci.yml")'`
- GitHub Actions job not run locally

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(simulator): extract fault scheduling engine

Summary:
- move scripted and scheduled fault orchestration out of
  EtherCAT.Simulator into Runtime.FaultEngine
- classify simulator call failures as :not_found, :timeout, or
  {:server_exit, reason} instead of collapsing crashes into
  :not_found
- add a regression test that proves the public API distinguishes a
  real server crash from an absent simulator

Rationale:
- keep EtherCAT.Simulator focused on the process facade and core
  state transitions instead of accumulating all fault machinery
- preserve fault visibility for test infrastructure by surfacing
  real simulator crashes as explicit API errors

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/simulator.ex
  lib/ethercat/simulator/runtime/fault_engine.ex
  test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs
  test/ethercat/simulator_redundancy_test.exs
  test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(tools): implement Phase 3 default tool suite (#28)

* feat(tools): implement Phase 3 default tool suite

Add 8 built-in tools covering filesystem, shell, and web operations:
- Read, Write, Edit - File operations with workspace scoping
- Glob, Grep - File discovery and content search
- Bash - Shell command execution
- WebSearch, WebFetch - DuckDuckGo search and content extraction

Safety features:
- ToolPolicy for capability switches (all enabled by default)
- Workspace path restriction (no escaping cwd)
- Binary file detection and rejection
- Size limits and truncation with UTF-8 boundary handling

CLI integration:
- Add --deny-write, --deny-bash, --deny-network flags
- Settings file support for tool policy
- Compact JSON result envelopes

New dependencies: ddgs>=4.0.0, trafilatura>=2.0.0

* fix(tools): repair policy overrides and web search wiring

Summary:
- make --deny-* flags override stored tool policy in chat mode
- preserve stored allow_* settings when init rewrites settings.json
- import DDGS from the installed ddgs package and add regression tests
- sync uv.lock with the branch's added web tool dependencies

Rationale:
- one-off deny flags must reliably narrow capabilities even when users
  have permissive defaults stored locally
- init --force should not silently drop persisted tool policy and widen
  later chat runs
- web_search must import the package actually declared by the project

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): restore HTML parsing in web_fetch

Summary:
- rename the HTML helper parameter to avoid shadowing the html
  module used for entity decoding
- add a regression test covering HTML title extraction and body
  extraction in web_fetch

Rationale:
- web_fetch raised ToolExecutionError for normal HTML pages because
  html.unescape resolved against a string parameter
- keeping the fix narrow matches the requested scope and leaves the
  other review findings untouched

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_web_tools.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(tools): align tool boundaries with cwd semantics

Summary:
- skip glob and grep entries whose resolved targets escape the
  workspace via symlinks
- clarify that bash starts in cwd but is not sandboxed to it
- add local filesystem regression tests and update the Phase 3 tool
  design notes and system prompt wording

Rationale:
- filesystem discovery tools should treat cwd as both their starting
  point and their effective boundary
- bash cannot honestly promise workspace confinement, so the docs
  need to describe its real contract instead of implying a sandbox

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_local_fs_tools.py tests/unit/test_web_tools.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

ci(repo): add raw simulator CI job

Summary:
- add a separate Ubuntu raw-simulator job to GitHub Actions
- create veth pairs for single-link and redundant raw tests
- grant CAP_NET_RAW to beam.smp before running raw-tagged tests

Rationale:
- raw simulator coverage needs AF_PACKET access and veth-backed links,
  which should stay isolated from the normal test job
- a dedicated job keeps low-level networking failures readable and makes
  it easier to validate raw transport support on GitHub-hosted runners

commit.message:
docs(simulator): require repair plans and fix provenance

Summary:
- extend the simulator README so scenario work must also call out
  master API and test-helper API pressure
- require fault descriptions, short repair plans, and follow-through on
  implementing and verifying fixes when a scenario finds a real bug
- document commit expectations so fixes point back to the triggering
  simulator regression test

Rationale:
- the prior README described how to reproduce simulator regressions, but
  it did not explicitly require the full loop from diagnosis through
  repair and traceable history
- making API pressure and fix provenance explicit should push scenario
  work toward cleaner public surfaces, better helper APIs, and commits
  that explain which regression exposed the problem

Validation:
- not run; documentation-only change

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(simulator): add redundant raw topology support

Summary:
- add simulator topology state with linear and redundant modes
- support ingress-aware raw endpoints and dual raw supervision
- add redundant simulator tests, raw integration harness, and missing-veth warnings

Rationale:
- make redundant bus behavior testable through the simulator without changing
  slave runtime semantics
- model healthy secondary passthrough and deterministic single-break behavior
  at the router boundary where link topology belongs

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs test/ethercat/simulator_redundancy_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs --include raw_socket
- mix test test/integration/simulator --max-failures 1
- mix docs.fresh

feat(simulator): add raw socket endpoint

Summary:
- add `EtherCAT.Simulator.RawSocket` as a single-link AF_PACKET
  EtherCAT endpoint for the simulator runtime
- wire `raw: [interface: ...]` through simulator supervision, info,
  and start-option validation
- document the raw transport path and add loopback raw-socket tests
  behind the `:raw_socket` ExUnit tag

Rationale:
- this is the first milestone toward simulator-backed raw-link and
  eventual redundant-link coverage
- keeping the raw path on the existing in-memory simulator core
  validates the transport boundary before adding topology logic
- the tagged loopback smoke test exercises real EtherType 0x88A4
  traffic without making the default test suite privilege-dependent

Validation:
- mix docs.fresh

- mix test test/ethercat/simulator_test.exs --include raw_socket

commit.message:
chore: CIをpnpm前提へ更新する

GitHub Actionsの依存解決と実行コマンドをpnpm workspace前提へ揃える。
Playwrightセットアップと補助指示もpnpmベースへ更新する。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(circuit-breaker): add reset_circuit_breakers bundle field for manual reset (#21)

When auto_reset_after_minutes=0, there was no way to reset a tripped
circuit breaker without restarting nginx workers. This adds an optional
reset_circuit_breakers field to the policy bundle: when present, apply()
calls circuit_breaker.reset() for each listed limit_key before activating
the new bundle.

Deliberately requires a new bundle deploy (not a direct admin API) so
the free/open-core path has a working but inconvenient reset mechanism.
A SaaS control plane can make this one-click by issuing a bundle with
the reset field pre-populated.

Closes #17

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(redundant): merge split logical replies correctly

Summary:
- document the healthy-ring and single-break redundant path model
- keep the original sent payload while awaiting redundant replies
- merge logical datagram data against the sent payload so
  complementary halves survive a single break
- add redundant bus regressions for reverse-path passthrough and
  split logical LRW replies

Rationale:
- the old merge logic chose a whole datagram by WKC and could drop
  valid process-data updates from one side of a broken ring
- the implementation now matches the documented primary and secondary
  path behavior more closely without changing the bus scheduler model

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/bus/link/redundant.ex test/ethercat/bus_test.exs
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

@EndLog no longer terminates @BeginExample, and vice versa.

commit.message:
test(bus): cover realtime priority under load

Summary:
- add a bus stress test with a sustained reliable backlog
- verify queued realtime work continues to preempt that backlog
- check a late-arriving realtime request still runs before the batched
  reliable frame

Rationale:
- the existing priority test covered a narrow backlog shape but not a
  loaded queue with repeated realtime dispatches
- the scheduler contract lives in `EtherCAT.Bus`, so this is the right
  layer to pin before adding broader simulator coverage

Validation:
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor(bus): clarify transaction aging semantics

Summary:
- document what `realtime` and `reliable` mean at the bus layer
- rename the realtime age budget from `deadline_us` to `stale_after_us`
- route `Bus.do_call/3` through the existing `safe_call/3` helper

Rationale:
- keep the current API shape while making the scheduling semantics easier
  to understand
- avoid implying an absolute deadline when the bus actually enforces a
  max queued age from submission time
- remove duplicated `:gen_statem.call` exit handling in the bus facade

Validation:
- mix format lib/ethercat/bus.ex lib/ethercat/bus/submission.ex test/support/fake_bus.ex
- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs test/ethercat/fake_bus_test.exs

commit.message:
refactor(bus): expose richer runtime diagnostics

Summary:
- factor the repeated bus call wrappers through one private `safe_call/3`
- extend `Bus.info/1` with queue depths, in-flight frame details,
  timeout count, and the last down reason
- retain the last transition-down reason in bus state and cover the
  new diagnostics in `bus_test.exs`

Rationale:
- the bus scheduler already had solid dispatch semantics, but its public
  diagnostics were too thin for a foundational process boundary
- `Bus.info/1` is the right place to surface queue and in-flight state
  without forcing callers to infer failures from logs alone

Validation:

- mix test test/ethercat/bus_test.exs

- remove the `.tmp/symphony` gitlink from version control
- keep the earlier skill catalog update intact without vendoring the subrepo

Rationale:
- avoid publishing a temporary nested repository reference on `main`
- preserve a clean repository layout for clones and future syncs

Tests:
- not run (repository metadata change only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Made-with: Cursor

feat: enforce beta generation quota across backend and UI

backend:
- add a quota service module to read profile limits and increment generations used
- enforce quota checks before websocket generation and return quota_exhausted errors when empty
- increment profiles.generations_used after successful done events with non-blocking error logging

tests:
- add websocket handler tests for quota exhaustion, successful increments, and no increment on pipeline failure
- update generation pipeline test setup to stub quota checks and avoid external profile access

frontend:
- load profile quota from Supabase on app load and refresh quota after successful generation
- show remaining generation quota in the top bar and on the questionnaire generate screen
- disable Generate Architecture at zero remaining and show beta exhaustion messaging
- stop generating state when websocket error responses arrive

Closes #12

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: Shorebird 수동 빌드 flavor 입력 추가

- /build/shorebird 에 flavor form input을 추가
- dev, stg, stage, prd, prod 값을 지원하도록 정규화
- 값이 없으면 SHOREBIRD_PATCH_FLAVOR 또는 prod를 fallback으로 사용
